description: Global Warming Potentials (GWP) from IPCC Assessment Reports. GWP values convert non-CO2 gases to CO2 equivalents. AR5 (2013) is currently most widely used; AR6 (2021) is becoming the new standard.
source: IPCC Fifth Assessment Report (AR5, 2013) and Sixth Assessment Report (AR6, 2021)
notes: GWP-100 values (100-year time horizon). The GHG Protocol and most frameworks currently reference AR5 values. ESRS and ISSB are transitioning to AR6.

gases.name: Methane
gases.notes: Fossil methane GWP in AR6 is 29.8 (includes climate-carbon feedback). Non-fossil methane is 27.0. Value shown is for fossil methane without feedback per AR5 convention.

gases.name: Nitrous oxide
gases.notes: Long-lived gas. Important for agriculture and industrial processes.

gases.name: Sulfur hexafluoride
gases.notes: Most potent GHG. Used in electrical switchgear. Very long atmospheric lifetime.

gases.name: 1,1,1,2-Tetrafluoroethane
gases.notes: Common refrigerant in vehicle air conditioning. Being phased down under Kigali Amendment. Critical for GCC due to high cooling demand.

gases.name: Difluoromethane
gases.notes: Replacement refrigerant for R-410A. Lower GWP alternative used in newer AC systems.

gases.name: Pentafluoroethane
gases.notes: Component of R-410A and R-407C blends.

gases.name: R-410A (blend)
gases.notes: Most common commercial/residential AC refrigerant globally and in GCC. Blend of HFC-32 (50%) and HFC-125 (50%). Kigali phase-down applies.

gases.name: R-407C (blend)
gases.notes: Zeotropic blend used in AC systems. Common retrofit refrigerant.

gases.name: Carbon tetrafluoride (Perfluoromethane)
gases.notes: Extremely long atmospheric lifetime. Used in aluminium smelting (relevant for GCC aluminium industry).

gases.name: Hexafluoroethane (Perfluoroethane)
gases.notes: Used in aluminium smelting and semiconductor manufacturing.
